posts.raw_content:
There has been much misleading on this forum about Thomas Massie ever since he pursued the Epstein issue while Trump wanted to bury it. Thomas not being bought by AIPAC may also be a reason. 

Here is information about his support of  immigration control and border security measures. 

https://thomasmassie.com/#ISSUES

> Secure our Border and Build the Wall.
> I support President Trump’s efforts to stop illegal immigration and voted to fund the wall for the full amount requested by the President.

## Overall voting record consistent with immigration control

https://grades.numbersusa.com/gradecard/11580/119

- NumbersUSA, an organization that advocates for reduced immigration and stricter enforcement, assigns Massie high grades across recent Congresses (e.g., 115th and 118th), reflecting a voting record aligned with immigration-control and border-security priorities.

**Here are documented examples, with direct quotes and links, of Rep. Thomas Massie’s support for immigration control and border security measures since early 2016.**

## 2014–2016: Blaming DACA for the surge and supporting restrictions (found in a 2018 post recapping his 2014 stance)

- **Quote:** “President Obama’s 2012 DACA program led directly to the current surge of illegal immigration into the United States by encouraging immigrants to put themselves in harm’s way and break the law. Passage of this bill will cost the taxpayer nothing, yet it will send a clear message to Central American parents that amnesty will not be provided to new arrivals.”
- **Source:** This quote appears in an official House write-up of H.R. 5272 (a bill to block new deportation deferrals) and reflects Massie’s stated rationale at the time. A public copy of the text appears in a House.gov email item that references Massie’s statement: https://massie.house.gov/news/email/show.aspx?ID=XBUDI4ZV5GH3SIWJUNWQMYZ6JI

## 2016: Co-sponsoring a resolution recognizing states’ authority to repel an “illegal alien invasion”

- **Quote (from his April 8, 2024 newsletter looking back at prior activity):** “Cosponsored a House resolution recognizing the constitutional authority of states to repel the illegal alien invasion.”
- **Source:** Thomas Massie’s official House newsletter, April 8, 2024: https://massie.house.gov/news/email/show.aspx?ID=LJCWZJNIK2RRG3FOVIMVM2ON4M

## June 10, 2025: Explicit call for deportation of illegal entrants

- **Quote:** “If you enter this country illegally, you broke the law and you should be deported. Also, enforcing these laws should not require bankrupting the country. Can we all agree on these two things?”
- **Source:** Rep. Thomas Massie’s official Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/RepThomasMassie/posts/if-you-enter-this-country-illegally-you-broke-the-law-and-you-should-be-deported/1257761435706514

## March 6, 2025: Voting for the Shut Down Sanctuary Policies Act in committee

- **Quote:** “I voted for the Shut Down Sanctuary Policies Act of 2026 in the House Judiciary Committee. Sanctuary cities endanger citizens & encourage lawlessness. This common-sense bill protects local law enforcement when they choose to assist federal immigration authorities.”
- **Source:** Rep. Thomas Massie’s official Facebook page (post dated March 6, 2025): https://www.facebook.com/RepThomasMassie/posts/i-voted-for-the-shut-down-sanctuary-policies-act-of-2026-in-the-house-judiciary-/1473000644182591

## March 6, 2025: Supporting immigration enforcement with due-process guardrails

- **Quote:** “I support immigration enforcement and deportation of illegal immigrants, but judicial warrants should be obtained before entering homes.”
- **Source:** Rep. Thomas Massie’s official Facebook page (post dated March 6, 2025): https://www.facebook.com/RepThomasMassie/posts/today-in-the-house-judiciary-committee-i-voted-for-a-bipartisan-amendment-to-ens/1472451710904151

## September 11, 2024: Condemning the “Biden-Harris open border policy”

- **Quote:** “The Biden-Harris open border policy has had devastating consequences. … Warning: it’s hard not to cry if you listen to these stories of victims of illegal immigrants.”
- **Source:** Rep. Thomas Massie’s official Facebook page (post dated September 11, 2024), sharing Judiciary Committee victim testimony: https://www.facebook.com/RepThomasMassie/posts/warning-its-hard-not-to-cry-if-you-listen-to-these-stories-of-victims-of-illegal/1062191401930186

## 2018: Voting for border security funding

- In 2018, Heritage Action designated a key vote on “Border Security Funding in the Continuing Resolution.” Massie’s vote is recorded as supporting that funding. See the vote entry and record (requires selecting the 115th Congress session): https://heritageaction.com/scorecard/votes/h472-2018
